title: Intracellular neuromelanin quantification
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3o7ll","text":"Protocol for quantifying intracellular neuromelanin in coronal sections of the rat brain in unstained sections (NM OD).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Calculate the optical density of neuromelanin for each cell by subtracting the background optical density from cell mean grey values
Take pictures of different cells in the pigmented area from unstained sections using the Zeiss Axio Imager.M2 microscope coupled to a camera with 100X oil objective.
Calibrate grey mean values for standard optical density values
With the free hand selections tool, draw the intraneuronal neuromelanin pigment of a neuron and measure the optical density. Measure approximately 30-40 neurons per animal
Then, in the same image, trace the region of interest in a blank area and measure the optical density
creator: Ada Ledonne
